description: Plan lifecycle enforcement: validate plan JSON, enforce spec completeness, regenerate docs from canonical plans, switch verify to JSON-first, and migrate legacy plans

agents.name: task-manager-plan-lifecycle
agents.scope: Implement plan validation, spec-completeness checks, canonical JSON criteria helpers, deterministic markdown rendering, and refactor metadata persistence in task_manager.py.

agents.name: plan-lifecycle-tests
agents.scope: Add focused lifecycle tests for validation, approval/execute gating, spec checks, markdown rendering, plan criteria output, and refactor metadata.

agents.name: planning-surface-alignment
agents.scope: Align manager, planner, and planning-contract docs to the validated JSON-first lifecycle and backend-owned markdown renderer.

agents.name: legacy-plan-migration
agents.scope: Migrate existing data/plans artifacts, regenerate markdown from canonical JSON, and explicitly mark incomplete historical plans for backfill where required.

conflicts: scripts/task_manager.py remains single-owner work for the duration of the campaign.
Planning docs must follow backend primitive names and lifecycle semantics after AQ lands.
Legacy plan JSON and derived markdown should only be rewritten after validation and rendering are stable.
integration_steps: Implement backend lifecycle primitives in AQ before any skill-surface updates.
Use AR to lock backend behavior before migrating stored plan artifacts.
Run AS after AQ so the planning surfaces document the backend that exists, not the backend that is intended.
Run AT last so migrated plan artifacts are rendered from the final canonical schema.

conflict_zones.reason: all lifecycle enforcement tickets converge in one backend file
planning surfaces must stay synchronized with backend lifecycle semantics
legacy artifact migration must use one renderer and one validator

plan_elements.goal_statement: Make the plan lifecycle in scripts/task_manager.py enforceable rather than advisory by adding approval-time validation, spec completeness checks before launch, backend-owned markdown rendering from canonical JSON, JSON-first criteria access for /manager verify, and explicit metadata/backfill handling for legacy plan artifacts.
exit_criteria: plan validate [plan-id] [--json] exists, is non-mutating, and reports structured errors and warnings.
plan approve fails invalid plans by default and plan execute refuses non-approved plans.
Validation rejects missing required plan elements, unknown dependencies, duplicate agent IDs, and duplicate file ownership.
Generated spec templates include exit criteria and machine-detectable completeness markers.
run refuses incomplete generated specs with unresolved placeholders or missing exit criteria.
Every plan mutation writes both data/plans/{plan-id}.json and a regenerated docs/campaign-{plan-id}-{slug}.md.
/manager verify is documented to consume JSON plan criteria first and treat markdown as derived drift output.
Existing data/plans/plan-001.json through plan-007.json are migrated or explicitly marked for backfill.
Automated tests cover validation, approval/execute gating, spec completeness, JSON-to-markdown rendering, plan criteria output, and refactor metadata round-trip.

agent_roster.scope: Implement plan validation, spec-completeness checks, canonical JSON criteria helpers, deterministic markdown rendering, and refactor metadata persistence in task_manager.py.

conflict_zone_analysis: scripts/task_manager.py remains single-owner work for the duration of the campaign.
Planning docs must follow backend primitive names and lifecycle semantics after AQ lands.
Legacy plan JSON and derived markdown should only be rewritten after validation and rendering are stable.
integration_points: Implement backend lifecycle primitives in AQ before any skill-surface updates.
Use AR to lock backend behavior before migrating stored plan artifacts.
Run AS after AQ so the planning surfaces document the backend that exists, not the backend that is intended.
Run AT last so migrated plan artifacts are rendered from the final canonical schema.
schema_changes: Add approval-time validation semantics for required plan fields.
Add top-level plan metadata for planner kind, provenance, and refactor-specific fields.
Add deterministic markdown rendering from canonical JSON plan data.
Add explicit legacy/backfill markers for historical plan artifacts.

risk_assessment.risk: Approval strictness immediately invalidates historical plans.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Migrate or explicitly mark legacy plans rather than silently treating them as valid.

risk_assessment.risk: task_manager.py changes break current plan persistence or command flows.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Keep the file single-owner and add lifecycle tests immediately after backend work.

risk_assessment.risk: Backend markdown rendering overwrites handwritten details not captured in JSON.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Settle canonical fields before migration and use AT to backfill preserved intent explicitly.

risk_assessment.risk: Planning docs drift from backend semantics again.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Run AS only after AQ and keep all planning surfaces under one owner.

verification_strategy: renderer smoke: regenerate docs/campaign-{plan-id}-{slug}.md from canonical JSON
documentation_updates: Update .codex/skills/manager/SKILL.md so verify is documented as JSON-first and markdown-secondary.
Update .codex/skills/planner/SKILL.md for backend validation and renderer ownership.
Update .codex/skills/planning-contract.md to make markdown's derived status explicit in the lifecycle section.
Regenerate migrated docs/campaign-*.md files from canonical JSON where appropriate.

example_note: Historical source artifact imported from source-repo development. Paths and commands may be source-specific and are not package-neutral install guidance.
